description: KanaDojo is an aesthetic, community-made platform for learning Japanese inspired by Duolingo and Monkeytype.
instructions: To begin, pick a dojo below and start training now!

kana.header: Welcome to the kana (hiragana and katakana) dojo!
kana.content1: This is the place where you can learn and practice the two core syllabaries of Japanese - Hiragana and Katakana.
kana.content2: To begin, select at least 1 set of characters, select or change the training mode, then hit <b/> below and start training now!

kanji.content1: This is the place where you can learn and practice the main component of the Japanese writing system - the kanji characters.
kanji.content2: To begin, select at least 1 level, select your training mode, then hit <b/> below and start training!
kanji.content3: New: click on a kanji character to find out more about it on <a/>

vocabulary.content1: This is the place where you can learn and practice the most common words used in day-to-day Japanese.
vocabulary.content2: To begin, select at least 1 level, select your training mode, then hit <b/> below and start training!
vocabulary.content3: New: click on a word to find out more about it on <a/>

jlptMenu.content: The test consists of five levels, with N1 being the highest level and N5 the lowest. N1 covers kanji characters and vocabulary that allow the user to understand Japanese used in business, academia and other high-level environments, N2 covers kanji characters and vocabulary allow the user to have the ability to understand Japanese used in everyday situations, and in a variety of circumstances to a proficient degree, N3 represents vocabulary that allows the user to understand Japanese used in everyday situations to an intermediate degree, N4 represents the ability to understand some basic real-life Japanese, and N5 represents the ability to understand some simple Japanese at a basic level.

groupMenu.header: About the Japanese Language Proficiency Test (JLPT)
groupMenu.content:
JLPT (short for Japanese-Language Proficiency Test) is a standardized criterion-referenced test to evaluate and certify Japanese language proficiency for non-native speakers.
The JLPT vocabulary lists categorize kanji characters and words based on their appearance in the Japanese Language Proficiency Test (JLPT). The test has five levels, from N5 (easiest) to N1 (most difficult), each requiring knowledge of a specific set of kanji. N5 and N4 focus on basic kanji and words used in day-to-day life, while N3, N2, and N1 introduce more complex and specialized characters and vocabulary. Originally designed for JLPT preparation, these vocabulary are also widely used by learners all over the world to study Japanese effectively and systematically.

wordClassMenu.content: Word types categorize words based on their function and meaning in a sentence. Nouns name people, places, things, or ideas - like "dog", "city", "happiness", and so on. Adjectives describe or modify nouns, giving details about certain qualities of a noun - like "happy", "beautiful", "tall", "blue". Verbs express actions, states, or occurrences - like "run", "think", "exist". And finally, adverbs modify verbs, adjectives or even other adverbs, often indicating manner, time, or degree - like "quickly", "slowly", "poorly", "very", "yesterday", and so on.
